title: How to Resize an Image Online – Free & Private Tool
description: Learn how to resize any image quickly and privately with our free online image resizer. No signup required.
h1: How to Resize an Image Online
content:
Resizing images is essential for websites, social media, and email. Our free online resizer makes it instant and private.

Why Resize Images?

  • Optimize for web: Smaller images load faster.
  • Social media: Each platform has ideal dimensions.
  • Email attachments: Reduce file size to meet limits.

Step-by-Step Guide

  1. Go to the Image Resizer tool.
  2. Click 'Choose File' and select an image.
  3. Enter desired width and height (lock aspect ratio optional).
  4. Click 'Download' – your resized image is ready.

title: Resize Image for Instagram – Perfect Post & Story Dimensions
description: Learn the ideal Instagram image sizes for posts, stories, and profile pictures. Use our resizer to fit instantly.
content:
Instagram has specific size requirements. Using wrong dimensions can crop your image. Our tool helps you get it right.

Recommended Sizes

  • Square post: 1080x1080
  • Portrait: 1080x1350
  • Stories: 1080x1920

How to Resize

  1. Go to Image Resizer.
  2. Upload your image.
  3. Enter your target dimensions.
  4. Download and upload to Instagram.

title: Resize Image for YouTube Thumbnail – Best Size (1280x720)
description: Create eye-catching YouTube thumbnails with the perfect size. Our resizer makes it easy.
content:
YouTube recommends 1280x720 pixels for thumbnails. Use our tool to resize any image to that exact size.

Steps

  1. Open Image Resizer.
  2. Upload your image.
  3. Set width 1280, height 720.
  4. Download and upload to YouTube.

title: Resize Image for Facebook Cover – Exact Dimensions (851x315)
description: Get the perfect Facebook cover photo size with our free resizer. Ensure your cover looks great on all devices.
content:
Facebook cover photos display at 851x315 pixels on desktop and 640x360 on mobile. Resize your image to fit perfectly.

How to Resize

  1. Go to Image Resizer.
  2. Upload your image.
  3. Enter 851 for width and 315 for height.
  4. Download and set as your cover.

title: Resize Image for Email – Reduce Attachment Size
description: Learn how to resize images for email attachments to stay under size limits. Fast and private.
content:
Email services often limit attachments to 25MB. Resizing reduces file size without losing quality.

Steps

  1. Use Image Resizer.
  2. Upload your image.
  3. Reduce dimensions (e.g., 800x600).
  4. Download and attach to email.

title: How to Compress an Image Online – Reduce File Size Free
description: Compress images without losing quality using our free online tool. Private, fast, and no signup.

content:

Why Compress?

Step-by-Step

content:

title: Compress Image for Website – Improve Page Speed
description: Learn how to compress images for web to boost loading speed and SEO. Free tool.
content:
Large images slow down websites. Compress them to improve user experience.

How to Compress

  1. Use Image Compressor.
  2. Upload image.
  3. Adjust quality (80% is a good start).
  4. Download and upload to your site.

title: Compress Image Without Losing Quality – Best Settings
description: Find the perfect balance between file size and image quality. Our tool lets you adjust compression level.
content:
You can reduce file size significantly while keeping good visual quality. Experiment with the quality slider.

Tips

  • For photos, quality 80-85% is often enough.
  • For graphics, PNG may be better.

content:
JPG doesn't support transparency, but PNG does. After conversion, you can edit the PNG in any image editor to add transparency.

Steps

  2. Open in editor, remove background, save as PNG.

h1: Convert JPG to PNG for Web
content: PNG is ideal for logos, icons, and images requiring transparency. JPG is better for photos. Use our converter when you need PNG.

h1: Convert JPG to PNG for Printing
content: For high-quality prints, PNG is a good choice because it doesn't introduce artifacts. Convert your images before sending to print.
